_meta.description: Deterministic scope resolution for doc lock, follow-up inheritance, and broad-versus-single scope decisions.

rules.trigger: Query explicitly mentions a filename or exact doc phrase
rules.explanatory: A filename reference must anchor to an attached document and lock that document.

rules.trigger: Follow-up query without a new document reference while lock is active
rules.check: context.docLock.active == true AND context.currentQuery.docRef == null AND context.conversation.turnsSinceDocRef <= 4
rules.explanatory: Keep single-doc scope for conversational follow-up unless user explicitly widens scope.

rules.trigger: Compare operator is used with only one explicit doc in scope
rules.explanatory: Cross-document compare requires explicit anchors for both sides unless user asked global trend comparison.

rules.trigger: Query includes 'all'/'any' and asks broad discovery
rules.check: context.intentFamily == 'discovery' OR context.queryText.matches('(across all|across any|list all|em todos|todos os)')
rules.explanatory: Discovery and broad scan should ignore single-doc lock unless explicitly contraindicated.

rules.trigger: Query uses a document alias that does not map to a unique attached doc
rules.explanatory: Require explicit disambiguation before applying scope.

rules.trigger: Query references a sheet/slide/page while no single doc lock exists
rules.explanatory: Use top retrieval candidate with highest location-signal confidence, but remain explicit in response.

rules.trigger: Query introduces a period not mentioned in existing lock context
rules.explanatory: Keep doc lock but change period boundary explicitly.

rules.trigger: Clarifying follow-up asks for the previous answer location context
rules.explanatory: Preserve scope, but widen retrieval window around prior location.

templates.text: Use machine-actionable policy fallback when a specific template is missing in scope_resolution_rules.
